experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with only a Z stack near the bottom center-right. The active J is near the top in its flat orientation. Goal is to place pieces efficiently to build a flat stack and avoid creating holes. Since the board is open and there is no immediate threat, the best sub-goal is to move the J toward the left wall where it can later be dropped cleanly without interfering with the bottom Z shape, preserving central space for upcoming S, I, O, L pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would place the J quickly but likely in the center above the Z, which is less flexible; soft_drop advances downward without improving horizontal placement; rotations are unnecessary now on an empty board; right moves toward the Z and is less desirable. Therefore moving left is the strongest setup action.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top at columns 4-6. The stack is low and uneven, with useful space on the left/center; there is no immediate threat, so the sub-goal is to start moving this L toward a good landing zone rather than locking it high with a hard drop. From the spawn, shifting left begins aligning the L toward the left-side contour above the existing T/J structure. Right would move away from that, rotations are premature before horizontal alignment, and dropping now would commit too early.

A right rotation changes the L into a vertical shape that is generally more flexible for slotting into the left-center contours on subsequent moves, advancing toward a cleaner placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top in rotation 1, with a relatively low, uneven stack below. The center-left surface has a shallow notch around columns 3-5 above the existing T/O/I structure, and there is no immediate danger since the board is still low. Goal now is to place the L efficiently to keep the stack flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming T,S,O,J pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current column, likely less controlled; rotations could shape-fit later but first horizontal positioning is most valuable; soft_drop gains little without alignment. Moving left shifts the L toward the center-left notch where it can likely be dropped into a cleaner fit on subsequent turns than staying at column 4 or moving right into emptier but less useful space. So the best immediate action is left.

The surface below has a central-left T/O/I structure and open space on both sides, so there is no immediate threat requiring a fast drop. My goal now is to improve placement flexibility and line up the L for a cleaner fit on the left side rather than commit to a premature hard drop. Moving left starts bringing the piece toward the left-side contour where L pieces commonly tuck, while preserving all later rotation/drop options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally near the top at row 2, columns 4-7. Surface below has a deep vertical well around column 5 above the T at row 10 and open air down several rows, making a vertical I valuable. Goal is to place the I to reduce holes and potentially prepare future line clears with upcoming O,T,J,S. Among actions, rotate_right converts the I to vertical immediately while keeping it aligned near that central well; left/right would shift the horizontal bar without improving fit yet, and dropping now horizontally would create overhangs. So best immediate sub-goal is rotate to vertical.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
